pairs.question: When was the 2019 Indian general election held in Kerala?
pairs.answer: The 2019 Indian general election in Kerala was held on 23 April 2019.

pairs.question: Which political alliance won the majority of seats in Kerala during the 2019 Indian general election?
pairs.answer: The United Democratic Front (UDF), led by the Indian National Congress, won 19 out of 20 seats in Kerala during the 2019 Indian general election.

pairs.question: What was the vote share of the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP)-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA) in Kerala during the 2019 elections?
pairs.answer: The BJP-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA) gathered around 15% of the total polled votes in Kerala during the 2019 Indian general election.

pairs.question: How did the Left Democratic Front (LDF)'s performance in the 2019 Kerala elections compare to its 2014 performance?
pairs.answer: In the 2019 elections, the LDF won only 1 seat in Kerala, with a vote share of 36%, which was a decrease from its 40% vote share in the 2014 general elections.

pairs.question: What were some of the key issues discussed during the 2019 Kerala election campaign?
pairs.answer: Key issues discussed during the 2019 Kerala election campaign included the Sabarimala Temple Protests, the impact of demonetization, and the implementation of the Goods and Services Tax (GST), despite these policies being introduced years before the election.

pairs.question: What role did Rahul Gandhi play in the 2019 Kerala elections?
pairs.answer: Rahul Gandhi, the UDF's prime ministerial candidate, contested from Kerala in the 2019 elections. His presence was a significant factor in the UDF's victory, as it helped shift some of the LDF's core voters to the NDA through the Bharath Dharma Jana Sena (BDJS) alliance.

pairs.question: How did the BJP-led NDA gain representation in the central government despite not winning any seats in Kerala during the 2019 elections?
pairs.answer: The BJP-led NDA secured one ministerial berth in the Second Modi ministry through V. Muraleedharan, who was a Member of Parliament in the Rajya Sabha from Kerala.
